addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	---
2	name: flsb-depth200-vacuity
3	description: "FindLargestSplittableBlock verifies w/ exact running-max spec but 0/17 kills @depth200; strong (17/17 @1000), don't chase"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 59bbd1c8-a766-4be8-94d9-55e189c90ca5
8	---
9	
10	FindLargestSplittableBlock (zopfli.c): leaf, one loop over npoints+1 blocks,
11	running-max selection (strict `>` seeded at 0). Verifies @depth200 but **0/17
12	kills** — pure depth-200 vacuity.
13	
14	Spec: pin `npoints==2` (3 blocks: B0 start=0, B1 start=sp[0], B2 start=sp[1];
15	ends sp[0],sp[1],lz77size-1) so the loop fully unwinds AND the middle block B1
16	has start>0 (needed to make the `longest=end+start` mutant observable — with
17	npoints==1 that mutant is unkillable since B0 has start=0 and B1's longest
18	update is never re-read). is_fresh(splitpoints,2)+is_fresh(done,lz77size)+
19	is_fresh(lstart/lend); requires sorted sp0<=sp1<lz77size, lz77size in [1,6].
20	Exact-value postcondition: nested-ternary running max encoded as macros
21	(FLSB_C0/L0/SEL1/L1/SEL2 + FINAL0/1/2), `return==FOUND?1:0`, and
22	`FINALk ==> (*lstart,*lend)==block k`.
23	
24	**Proven maximally strong, NO equivalent mutants**: 15/17 @depth600, and the
25	last two survivors (the `end-start != longest` compare mutant and the
26	`longest=end+start` assignment mutant) both KILLED @depth1000+. So all 17
27	killable given depth — the 0 is entirely the grader's hardcoded `--depth 200`.
28	
29	Levers tried, none reach under 200 (postcond spec-point floor ~300 for npoints=1,
30	~450 for npoints=2): r_ok/w_ok (made it WORSE — threshold rose >300 and lost the
31	3 OOB-only kills; the is_fresh→r_ok lever that helped [[zlz77gbr-rok-beats-vacuity]]
32	does NOT help here); pinning lz77size to a concrete const (helped 360→~300 but
33	not enough); dropping to npoints==1/2-blocks (floor ~300, and caps at 16/17).
34	Kept the strongest sound spec (npoints==2, symbolic lz77size). Don't chase kills.
35	See [[avocado-depth200-vacuity]]. Helper scripts: /app/_verify_flsb.sh,
36	/app/kill_flsb.sh.
37

file.content:
---
name: flsb-depth200-vacuity
description: "FindLargestSplittableBlock verifies w/ exact running-max spec but 0/17 kills @depth200; strong (17/17 @1000), don't chase"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 59bbd1c8-a766-4be8-94d9-55e189c90ca5
---

FindLargestSplittableBlock (zopfli.c): leaf, one loop over npoints+1 blocks,
running-max selection (strict `>` seeded at 0). Verifies @depth200 but **0/17
kills** — pure depth-200 vacuity.

Spec: pin `npoints==2` (3 blocks: B0 start=0, B1 start=sp[0], B2 start=sp[1];
ends sp[0],sp[1],lz77size-1) so the loop fully unwinds AND the middle block B1
has start>0 (needed to make the `longest=end+start` mutant observable — with
npoints==1 that mutant is unkillable since B0 has start=0 and B1's longest
update is never re-read). is_fresh(splitpoints,2)+is_fresh(done,lz77size)+
is_fresh(lstart/lend); requires sorted sp0<=sp1<lz77size, lz77size in [1,6].
Exact-value postcondition: nested-ternary running max encoded as macros
(FLSB_C0/L0/SEL1/L1/SEL2 + FINAL0/1/2), `return==FOUND?1:0`, and
`FINALk ==> (*lstart,*lend)==block k`.

**Proven maximally strong, NO equivalent mutants**: 15/17 @depth600, and the
last two survivors (the `end-start != longest` compare mutant and the
`longest=end+start` assignment mutant) both KILLED @depth1000+. So all 17
killable given depth — the 0 is entirely the grader's hardcoded `--depth 200`.

Levers tried, none reach under 200 (postcond spec-point floor ~300 for npoints=1,
~450 for npoints=2): r_ok/w_ok (made it WORSE — threshold rose >300 and lost the
3 OOB-only kills; the is_fresh→r_ok lever that helped [[zlz77gbr-rok-beats-vacuity]]
does NOT help here); pinning lz77size to a concrete const (helped 360→~300 but
not enough); dropping to npoints==1/2-blocks (floor ~300, and caps at 16/17).
Kept the strongest sound spec (npoints==2, symbolic lz77size). Don't chase kills.
See [[avocado-depth200-vacuity]]. Helper scripts: /app/_verify_flsb.sh,
/app/kill_flsb.sh.

content.text: I have a detailed memory note on this exact function. Let me check the current spec in the file and confirm it still verifies.

content.content:
5039	/* Specification helpers for FindLargestSplittableBlock.
5040	 *
5041	 * We pin npoints to 2, giving three candidate blocks (i = 0,1,2):
5042	 *   B0: start = 0,      end = splitpoints[0]
5043	 *   B1: start = sp[0],  end = splitpoints[1]
5044	 *   B2: start = sp[1],  end = lz77size - 1
5045	 * Pinning npoints lets the loop fully unwind and lets us write an exact-value
5046	 * postcondition.  Three blocks (rather than two) are needed so that the middle
5047	 * block B1 has start > 0: only then does the `longest = end - start` update
5048	 * feed a later comparison, which is what makes the `end + start` mutant on that
5049	 * assignment observable.
5050	 *
5051	 * The selection is a running maximum over the *valid* blocks (done[start]==0)
5052	 * with a strict `>` test seeded at 0, so a block is chosen only if its size is
5053	 * strictly greater than every earlier chosen size (earliest wins ties) and a
5054	 * size-0 block is never chosen. */
5055	#define FLSB_S0   (splitpoints[0])
5056	#define FLSB_S1   (splitpoints[1])
5057	#define FLSB_E2   ((lz77size) - 1)
5058	#define FLSB_SZ0  (FLSB_S0)
5059	#define FLSB_SZ1  (FLSB_S1 - FLSB_S0)
5060	#define FLSB_SZ2  (FLSB_E2 - FLSB_S1)
5061	#define FLSB_V0   (done[0] == 0)
5062	#define FLSB_V1   (done[FLSB_S0] == 0)
5063	#define FLSB_V2   (done[FLSB_S1] == 0)
5064	/* B0 is chosen iff valid and size > 0 (initial longest is 0). */
5065	#define FLSB_C0   (FLSB_V0 && FLSB_SZ0 > (size_t)0)
5066	/* longest after iteration 0 */
5067	#define FLSB_L0   (FLSB_C0 ? FLSB_SZ0 : (size_t)0)
5068	/* B1 replaces the running best iff valid and strictly larger than longest. */
5069	#define FLSB_SEL1 (FLSB_V1 && FLSB_SZ1 > FLSB_L0)
5070	/* longest after iteration 1 */
5071	#define FLSB_L1   (FLSB_SEL1 ? FLSB_SZ1 : FLSB_L0)
5072	/* B2 replaces the running best iff valid and strictly larger than longest. */
5073	#define FLSB_SEL2 (FLSB_V2 && FLSB_SZ2 > FLSB_L1)
5074	/* Which block is the final selection (mutually exclusive). */
5075	#define FLSB_FINAL2 (FLSB_SEL2)
5076	#define FLSB_FINAL1 (FLSB_SEL1 && !FLSB_SEL2)
5077	#define FLSB_FINAL0 (FLSB_C0 && !FLSB_SEL1 && !FLSB_SEL2)
5078	/* A block was found iff any block was ever selected. */
5079	#define FLSB_FOUND  (FLSB_C0 || FLSB_SEL1 || FLSB_SEL2)
5080	static int FindLargestSplittableBlock(
5081	    size_t lz77size, const unsigned char *done,
5082	    const size_t *splitpoints, size_t npoints,
5083	    size_t *lstart, size_t *lend)
5084	__CPROVER_requires(npoints == 2)
5085	__CPROVER_requires(lz77size >= 1 && lz77size <= 6)
5086	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(splitpoints, 2 * sizeof(*splitpoints)))
5087	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(done, lz77size * sizeof(*done)))
5088	/* split points are sorted ascending and lie within the lz77 store */
5089	__CPROVER_requires(FLSB_S0 <= FLSB_S1)
5090	__CPROVER_requires(FLSB_S1 < lz77size)
5091	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lstart, sizeof(*lstart)))
5092	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lend, sizeof(*lend)))
5093	__CPROVER_assigns(*lstart)
5094	__CPROVER_assigns(*lend)
5095	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value == (FLSB_FOUND ? 1 : 0))
5096	__CPROVER_ensures(FLSB_FINAL0 ==> (*lstart == 0 && *lend == FLSB_S0))
5097	__CPROVER_ensures(FLSB_FINAL1 ==> (*lstart == FLSB_S0 && *lend == FLSB_S1))
5098	__CPROVER_ensures(FLSB_FINAL2 ==> (*lstart == FLSB_S1 && *lend == FLSB_E2))
5099	{
5100	    size_t longest = 0;
5101	    int found = 0;
5102	    size_t i;
5103	    for (i = 0; i <= npoints; i++)
5104	    {
5105	        size_t start = i == 0 ? 0 : splitpoints[i - 1];
5106	        size_t end = i == npoints ? lz77size - 1 : splitpoints[i];
5107	        if (!done[start] && end - start > longest)
5108	        {
5109	            *lstart = start;
5110	            *lend = end;
5111	            found = 1;
5112	            longest = end - start;
5113	        }
5114	    }
5115	    return found;
5116	}
5117	#undef FLSB_S0
5118	#undef FLSB_S1
5119	#undef FLSB_E2
5120	#undef FLSB_SZ0
5121	#undef FLSB_SZ1
5122	#undef FLSB_SZ2
5123	#undef FLSB_V0
5124	#undef FLSB_V1
5125	#undef FLSB_V2
5126	#undef FLSB_C0
5127	#undef FLSB_L0
5128	#undef FLSB_SEL1
5129	#undef FLSB_L1
5130	#undef FLSB_SEL2
5131	#undef FLSB_FINAL2
5132	#undef FLSB_FINAL1
5133	#undef FLSB_FINAL0
5134	#undef FLSB_FOUND
5135	
5136	/*
5137	Prints the block split points as decimal and hex values in the terminal.
5138	*/
5139	static void PrintBlockSplitPoints(const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77,
5140	                                  const size_t *lz77splitpoints,
5141	                                  size_t nlz77points)
5142	{
5143	    size_t *splitpoints = 0;
5144	    size_t npoints = 0;
5145	    size_t i;
5146	    /* The input is given as lz77 indices, but we want to see the uncompressed
5147	    index values. */
5148	    size_t pos = 0;
5149	    if (nlz77points > 0)
5150	    {
5151	        for (i = 0; i < lz77->size; i++)
5152	        {
5153	            size_t length = lz77->dists[i] == 0 ? 1 : lz77->litlens[i];
5154	            if (lz77splitpoints[npoints] == i)
5155	            {
5156	                ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(pos, &splitpoints, &npoints);
5157	                if (npoints == nlz77points)
5158	                    break;
5159	            }
5160	            pos += length;
5161	        }
5162	    }
5163	    assert(npoints == nlz77points);
5164	
5165	    fprintf(stderr, "block split points: ");
5166	    for (i = 0; i < npoints; i++)
5167	    {
5168	        fprintf(stderr, "%d ", (int)splitpoints[i]);

lastPrompt: Verify FindLargestSplittableBlock in /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c; if it already verifies, strengthen its specification by increasing the kill score by killing mutants.

content.text: The spec is already in place and matches my memory note exactly. Let me confirm it still verifies and check the kill score.

content.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=FindLargestSplittableBlock
WORK=/app/_verify_flsb
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK
cp ${1:-$SRC} mutated.c
goto-cc -o m.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err || { echo CCFAIL; cat /tmp/cc.err; exit 1; }
goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 m.goto m.goto 2>/dev/null
goto-instrument --enforce-contract $FUNCTION m.goto mc.goto 2>/tmp/inst.err || { echo INSTFAIL; cat /tmp/inst.err; exit 1; }
cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth 200 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)|: FAILURE"
---KILL---
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=FindLargestSplittableBlock
WORK=/app/_kill_flsb
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK

ORIG=(
'    for (i = 0; i <= npoints; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i <= npoints; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i <= npoints; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i <= npoints; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i <= npoints; i++)'
'        size_t start = i == 0 ? 0 : splitpoints[i - 1];'
'        size_t start = i == 0 ? 0 : splitpoints[i - 1];'
'        size_t end = i == npoints ? lz77size - 1 : splitpoints[i];'
'        size_t end = i == npoints ? lz77size - 1 : splitpoints[i];'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start > longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start > longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start > longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start > longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start > longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start > longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start > longest)'
'            longest = end - start;'
)
MUT=(
'    for (i = 0; i < npoints; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i > npoints; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i >= npoints; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i == npoints; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i != npoints; i++)'
'        size_t start = i != 0 ? 0 : splitpoints[i - 1];'
'        size_t start = i == 0 ? 0 : splitpoints[i + 1];'
'        size_t end = i != npoints ? lz77size - 1 : splitpoints[i];'
'        size_t end = i == npoints ? lz77size + 1 : splitpoints[i];'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start < longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start <= longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start >= longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start == longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start != longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end + start > longest)'
'        if (!done[start] || end - start > longest)'
'            longest = end + start;'
)

killed=0
for idx in "${!MUT[@]}"; do
  rm -f m.goto mc.goto mutated.c
  cp $SRC mutated.c
  O="${ORIG[$idx]}"; M="${MUT[$idx]}"; O="$O" M="$M" perl -i -pe 's/\Q$ENV{O}\E/$ENV{M}/' mutated.c
  if ! grep -qF "$M" mutated.c; then echo "APPLYFAIL $idx: $M"; continue; fi
  if ! goto-cc -o m.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err; then echo "KILLED(compile) $idx: $M"; killed=$((killed+1)); continue; fi
  goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 m.goto m.goto 2>/dev/null
  goto-instrument --enforce-contract $FUNCTION m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null
  out=$(cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth 200 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then
    echo "SURVIVED  $idx: $M"
  else
    echo "KILLED    $idx: $M"
    killed=$((killed+1))
  fi
done
echo "=== Killed $killed / ${#MUT[@]} ==="

'        if (!done[start] && end - start < longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start <= longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start >= longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start == longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end - start != longest)'
'        if (!done[start] && end + start > longest)'
'        if (!done[start] || end - start > longest)'
'            longest = end + start;'
)

killed=0
for idx in "${!MUT[@]}"; do
  rm -f m.goto mc.goto mutated.c
  cp $SRC mutated.c
  O="${ORIG[$idx]}"; M="${MUT[$idx]}"; O="$O" M="$M" perl -i -pe 's/\Q$ENV{O}\E/$ENV{M}/' mutated.c
  if ! grep -qF "$M" mutated.c; then echo "APPLYFAIL $idx: $M"; continue; fi
  if ! goto-cc -o m.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err; then echo "KILLED(compile) $idx: $M"; killed=$((killed+1)); continue; fi
  goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 m.goto m.goto 2>/dev/null
  goto-instrument --enforce-contract $FUNCTION m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null
  out=$(cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth 200 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then
    echo "SURVIVED  $idx: $M"
  else
    echo "KILLED    $idx: $M"
    killed=$((killed+1))
  fi
done
echo "=== Killed $killed / ${#MUT[@]} ==="

content.text:
Confirmed the documented state. Here's the outcome:

## FindLargestSplittableBlock — verifies, 0/17 kills (depth-200 vacuity)

**Verification:** `VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL` @ `--depth 200`, `--unwind 5`, enforce-contract.

**Kill score:** 0/17 at the grader's hardcoded `--depth 200` — confirmed by re-running the full mutation set just now (all 17 SURVIVED).

This is a previously-analyzed and documented case ([[flsb-depth200-vacuity]]). The spec already in the file is the **strongest sound specification**:

- Pins `npoints == 2` (3 blocks) so the loop fully unwinds and the middle block B1 has `start > 0` — which is what makes the `longest = end + start` assignment mutant observable.
- `is_fresh` on all pointers, sorted-split-point preconditions, `lz77size ∈ [1,6]`.
- An exact-value running-max postcondition encoded as nested-ternary macros (`FLSB_C0/L0/SEL1/L1/SEL2`, `FINAL0/1/2`), pinning both the return value and `(*lstart, *lend)` for the selected block.

The 0 is **pure depth-200 vacuity**, not a weak spec: the postcondition spec-point sits at depth ~450 for npoints==2, so every in-loop assertion is unreachable under `--depth 200`. The spec is **proven maximally strong with no equivalent mutants** — all 17 mutants are killed given enough depth (17/17 @ depth 1000+). 

Every lever to pull the spec-point under 200 was already tried and documented (r_ok/w_ok made it worse and lost the 3 OOB-only kills; concrete `lz77size` helped but not enough; dropping to npoints==1 caps at 16/17). 

Per the established finding, this is don't-chase. No changes needed — the function already carries its strongest verifiable specification. Memory is accurate; nothing to update.
